economic_notes: Haisla Nation has extensive business interests including 16 limited partnerships and numerous joint ventures. Major LNG-related activities including Cedar LNG Export Development, LNG Canada Capacity, and various LNG joint ventures. North Pac Joint Venture generated $13.6M earnings. Atco Joint Venture generated $54.8M earnings. COVID-19 pandemic noted as potential future risk. Haisla Minors LNG trust fund maintained for members.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) Social Housing TCA amortized at CMHC-required rate rather than useful life per PSAS; (2) Auditors unable to obtain audited financials for business entities so investment in nation business entities cannot be verified. Accumulated surplus of $256.97M includes $183.2M in investments (Kitamaat LNG, Legacy Trust, etc.). The nation has 32+ business partnerships and joint ventures accounted for by modified equity method. Ottawa Trust lease agreement revenue of $18.6M is a significant recurring revenue source. Total expenses per statement of operations is $44,230,830 including all subsidiaries. Prior year revenues of $89.1M were unusually high due to $43.4M in fees and other income. Net financial assets not explicitly stated on face of statements as provided. Council remuneration disclosed as $463,859 total but individual ranges not broken out. Segment disclosure appears incomplete in the provided text.
